finding_results.notes: Three lines revised. (1) Priya's line about Lena's authority (line 177): replaced the clean maxim with a false-start self-correction ('whether she ordered it, that is not') followed by a concrete witness anchor ('The one by the cooler. He did not look at anyone first'). Retains formal register, no contractions, but now sounds like a person choosing words under fear rather than delivering polished commentary. (2) Priya's line about guard staffing (line 195): broke the neat two-clause reversal by inserting a physical pause (pulling at her cuff), a qualifying 'maybe', and a trailing admission of uncertainty ('I do not know which'). The observation still lands but can no longer be lifted as a standalone thesis. (3) Marcus's sprint-observation (line 257): replaced the composed four-clause summary with withdrawal-fragmented speech that routes through the family message ('She's asking if I'm eating'), a false start ('And outside that's—'), physical interruption (pressing heel of hand against eye), and a bitter projected detail ('Your dad's on a job, he doesn't call') instead of clean summary. All three revised lines still communicate the same information. None can be extracted as balanced thesis sentences without loss of their scene-specific anchors, hesitations, and qualifications.

summary: Single finding addressed across three dialogue sites. Priya's two lines in the Noor-Priya exchange were roughened with self-correction, concrete scene-witness detail, and trailing qualification while preserving her formal, low-contraction idiolect. Marcus's outside-world observation was fragmented through family-message callback, false start, physical interruption, and a bitter projected detail that replaces the neat thesis with withdrawal-specific spoken texture. No continuity, structural, or voice-signature changes elsewhere in the chapter.
